summary: Test suite has sound structure but is incomplete and cannot run. Unit tests (Bump-Version.Tests.ps1) provide reasonable coverage of core functions: Parse-SemanticVersion (5 cases including errors), Get-BumpType (7 cases), Bump-SemanticVersion (4 cases), Generate-Changelog (3 cases), and end-to-end scenarios (6 cases). However, critical files are missing: test fixtures (fixtures/patch-commits.txt, minor-commits.txt, major-commits.txt, breaking-footer-commits.txt, no-bump-commits.txt) are referenced but never created by tests, making them untestable without external files. The .github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml workflow file required by run-tests.ps1 is not provided. Rigor gaps: error scenarios under-tested (no tests for missing version files, permission errors, git log failures, malformed JSON); edge cases missing (special characters, multiline commit bodies, pre-release versions, empty fixture files). Design issues: fixtures created externally rather than in BeforeEach/BeforeAll (violates test isolation principle); workflow validation in run-tests.ps1 uses fragile string-matching on YAML instead of proper parsing; fixture content not specified or validated in assertions. The run-tests.ps1 harness demonstrates good integration testing approach (actionlint, act execution, output validation, 3-run limit), but requires missing workflow and fixtures. Overall, test code shows thoughtful design (clear naming, proper cleanup, parametric integration tests) but cannot execute as-is and needs programmatic fixture generation.
